NAME

log.c - 📝 Multi-level logging with terminal color support, file rotation, and async output

Data Structures

Macros

📝 Multi-level logging with terminal color support, file rotation, and async output

#define LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR( error, message, ...)

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  asciichat_set_errno_with_message(error, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, message, ##__VA_ARGS__);                     \


  static const char *msg_header = "CRITICAL LOGGING SYSTEM ERROR: ";                                                 \


  safe_fprintf(stderr, "%s %s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, msg_header), message);                             \


  int _log_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);                                                                        \


  platform_write(_log_fd, msg_header, strlen(msg_header));                                                           \


  platform_write(_log_fd, message, strlen(message));                                                                 \


  platform_write(_log_fd, "\n", 1);                                                                                  \


  backtrace_print_simple(0);                                                                                       \
} while (0)

char
  * format_message (const char * format, va_list args)


Definition at line 248 of file log/log.c.


248                                                        {
249   if (!format) {
250     return NULL;
251   }
252 
253   // First, determine the size needed
254   va_list args_copy;
255   va_copy(args_copy, args);
256   int size = safe_vsnprintf(NULL, 0, format, args_copy);
257   va_end(args_copy);
258 
259   if (size < 0) {
260     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
261     return NULL;
262   }
263 
264   // Allocate and format the message
265   char *message = SAFE_MALLOC(size + 1, char *);
266   int result = safe_vsnprintf(message, (size_t)size + 1, format, args);
267   if (result < 0) {
268     SAFE_FREE(message);
269     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
270     return NULL;
271   }
272 
273   return message;
274 }

size_t
  get_current_time_formatted (char * time_buf)


Definition at line 216 of file log/log.c.


216                                                   {
217   /* Get wall-clock time in nanoseconds */
218   uint64_t ts_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
219   // Extract seconds and nanoseconds from total nanoseconds
220   time_t seconds = (time_t)(ts_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT);
221   long nanoseconds = (long)(ts_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
222   struct tm tm_info;
223   platform_localtime(&seconds, &tm_info);
224   // Format the time part first
225   // strftime returns 0 on error, not negative (and len is size_t/unsigned)
226   size_t len = strftime(time_buf, 32, "%H:%M:%S", &tm_info);
227   if (len == 0 || len >= 32) {
228     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format time");
229     return 0;
230   }
231 
232   // Add microseconds manually (convert nanoseconds to microseconds for display)
233   long microseconds = nanoseconds / 1000;
234   if (microseconds < 0)
235     microseconds = 0;
236   if (microseconds > 999999)
237     microseconds = 999999;
238 
239   int result = safe_snprintf(time_buf + len, 32 - len, ".%06ld", microseconds);
240   if (result < 0 || result >= (int)(32 - len)) {
241     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format microseconds");
242     return 0;
243   }
244 
245   return len + (size_t)result;
246 }

const
  char * get_level_string_padded (log_level_t level)


Get padded level string for consistent alignment. Returns level
    names padded to 5 characters for visual alignment. Used consistently in both
    colored and plain text formatters to ensure grep pattern matching works
    correctly.




Parameters


level Log level


Returns


Padded level string (e.g., "INFO ", "WARN
  ", "DEBUG")


Definition at line 149 of file log/log.c.


149                                                        {
150   const char *result;
151   switch (level) {
152   case LOG_INFO:
153     result = "INFO "; // 5 chars: INFO + 1 space
154     break;
155   case LOG_WARN:
156     result = "WARN "; // 5 chars: WARN + 1 space
157     break;
158   case LOG_DEV:
159     result = "DEV  "; // 5 chars: DEV + 2 spaces
160     break;
161   case LOG_DEBUG:
162     result = "DEBUG"; // 5 chars: DEBUG (no padding needed)
163     break;
164   case LOG_ERROR:
165     result = "ERROR"; // 5 chars: ERROR (no padding needed)
166     break;
167   case LOG_FATAL:
168     result = "FATAL"; // 5 chars: FATAL (no padding needed)
169     break;
170   default:
171     result = "?????"; // Invalid level - return 5 question marks
172   }
173   // Verify length
174   if (strlen(result) != 5) {
175     fprintf(stderr, "ERROR: get_level_string_padded() returned non-5-char string: '%s' (len=%zu)\n", result,
176             strlen(result));
177   }
178   return result;
179 }

void
  log_console_impl (log_level_t level, const char * file, int line, const
  char * func, const char * message)


Definition at line 2042 of file log/log.c.


2042                                                                                                             {
2043   if (!message) {
2044     return;
2045   }
2046 
2047   int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(level);
2048   if (fd < 0) {
2049     return;
2050   }
2051 
2052   // Check if JSON output is enabled (json_file >= 0 means enabled)
2053   int json_fd = atomic_load_int(&g_log.json_file);
2054   bool use_json = (json_fd >= 0);
2055 
2056   if (use_json) {
2057     // Use async-safe JSON formatter (safe for signal handlers)
2058     log_json_async_safe(fd, level, file, line, func, message);
2059   } else {
2060     // Text output to console using platform_write_all to handle partial writes
2061     size_t msg_len = strlen(message);
2062     platform_write_all(fd, (const uint8_t *)message, msg_len);
2063     if (msg_len == 0 || message[msg_len - 1] != '\n') {
2064       platform_write_all(fd, (const uint8_t *)"\n", 1);
2065     }
2066   }
2067 }

void
  log_destroy (void )


Definition at line 622 of file log/log.c.


622                        {
623   // Destroy mmap logging first (if active)
624   if (log_mmap_is_active()) {
625     log_mmap_destroy();
626   }
627 
628   // Cleanup grep filter
629   grep_destroy();
630 
631   /* Mark logging system as shutdown FIRST (state machine transition)
632    * This signals worker threads to stop logging immediately before we free memory.
633    * This prevents TOCTOU race where a worker thread could see non-NULL format pointer
634    * after we've freed it but before lifecycle_shutdown() is called. */
635   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.lifecycle);
636 
637   // Cleanup custom format structures: All worker threads have been signaled to stop by this point.
638   // Safe to free templates now without use-after-free concerns.
639   if (g_log.format) {
640     log_template_free(g_log.format);
641     g_log.format = NULL;
642   }
643   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
644     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
645     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
646   }
647   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.has_custom_format, false);
648 
649   // Lock-free cleanup using atomic operations
650   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
651   if (old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
652     platform_close(old_file);
653   }
654   atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
655 
656   // Destroy rotation mutex
657   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle);
658 }

const
  char ** log_get_color_array (void )


Definition at line 1551 of file log/log.c.


1551                                        {
1552   init_terminal_capabilities();
1553 
1554   /* Colors should be initialized via log_system_init(); this is just a safety check */
1555   if (!g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized) {
1556     /* Fallback: initialize colors if not already done (should not happen after log_system_init) */
1557     log_init_colors();
1558   }
1559 
1560   /* Safety check: if colors are still not initialized, return NULL to prevent crashes from null pointers */
1561   if (!g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized) {
1562     return NULL;
1563   }
1564 
1565   /* Return the compiled color scheme based on terminal capabilities
1566    * codes_16, codes_256, codes_truecolor are now proper arrays of pointers (const char *[8]),
1567    * so we can safely cast them to const char **. */
1568   if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR) {
1569     return (const char **)g_log.compiled_colors.codes_truecolor;
1570   } else if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_256) {
1571     return (const char **)g_log.compiled_colors.codes_256;
1572   } else {
1573     return (const char **)g_log.compiled_colors.codes_16;
1574   }
1575 }

void
  * log_get_template (void )


Get the current log format template.


Returns


Opaque pointer to the current log format template (may be
  NULL if not set)


Returns the compiled log format template used by the logging
    system. This is used by platform code (e.g., backtrace formatting) to format
    log entries using the same template as the rest of the logging system.
    Returns void* (opaque) to avoid circular dependency with format.h.


Definition at line 2078 of file log/log.c.

2079   return (void *)g_log.format;
2080 }

void
  log_init (const char * filename, log_level_t level, bool
  force_stderr, bool use_mmap)


Definition at line 532 of file log/log.c.


532                                                                                          {
533   // Initialize rotation mutex (only operation that uses a mutex)
534   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX;
535   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync.mutex = &g_log.rotation_mutex;
536   lifecycle_init(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle, "log_rotation");
537 
538   // Set basic config using atomic stores
539   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.force_stderr, force_stderr);
540   bool preserve_terminal_output = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled);
541 
542   // Force logs to stderr if stdout is piped/redirected
543   // This prevents logs from contaminating piped/redirected output in snapshot/batch modes
544   // Per terminal.h: "force stderr when piped to prevent data corruption"
545   if (terminal_is_piped_output()) {
546     atomic_store_bool(&g_log.force_stderr, true);
547   }
548 
549   // Close any existing file (atomic load/store)
550   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
551   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle) && old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
552     platform_close(old_file);
553     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
554   }
555 
556   // Check LOG_LEVEL environment variable
557   const char *env_level_str = SAFE_GETENV("LOG_LEVEL");
558   if (env_level_str) {
559     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)parse_log_level_from_env());
560   } else {
561     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)level);
562   }
563 
564   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.level_manually_set, false);
565   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.current_size, 0);
566 
567   if (filename) {
568     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_log.filename, filename, sizeof(g_log.filename) - 1);
569 
570     if (use_mmap) {
571       // Lock-free mmap path - writes go to mmap'd file
572       asciichat_error_t mmap_result = log_mmap_init_simple(filename, 0);
573       if (mmap_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
574         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1); // No regular fd - using mmap for file output
575       } else {
576         // Mmap failed - use stderr only (atomic writes, lock-free)
577         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
578           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Mmap logging failed for %s, using stderr only (lock-free)\n", filename);
579         }
580         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
581         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
582       }
583     } else {
584       // Lock-free file I/O path - uses atomic write() syscalls
585       int fd = platform_open("log_file", filename, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
586       atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, (fd >= 0) ? fd : STDERR_FILENO);
587       if (fd < 0) {
588         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
589           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open log file: %s\n", filename);
590         }
591         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
592       }
593     }
594   } else {
595     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
596     g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
597   }
598 
599   /* Initialize default log format (NULL means use mode-specific default) */
600   log_set_format(NULL, false);
601 
602   /* Mark logging system as initialized (state machine transition) */
603   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
604     lifecycle_init(&g_log.lifecycle, "logging");
605   }
606 
607   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, preserve_terminal_output);
608 
609   // Reset terminal detection if needed
610   if (g_log.terminal_caps_initialized && !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
611     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = false;
612   }
613 
614   // Detect terminal capabilities
615   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
616

size_t
  log_recolor_plain_entry (const char * plain_line, char * colored_buf, size_t
  buf_size)


Recolor a plain (non-colored) log line with proper ANSI codes.
    Parses plain log format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in
    FUNC(): MESSAGE And recolors it with appropriate ANSI codes matching the
    colored format.


Definition at line 1790 of file log/log.c.


1790                                                                                            {
1791   if (!plain_line || !colored_buf || buf_size < 128) {
1792     return 0;
1793   }
1794 
1795   static char work_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE + 1024];
1796 
1797   // Parse format FIRST, regardless of color availability
1798   // Parse format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE
1799   const char *p = plain_line;
1800 
1801   // Extract timestamp [TIMESTAMP]
1802   if (*p != '[') {
1803     return 0; // Invalid format
1804   }
1805   p++;
1806   const char *timestamp_start = p;
1807 
1808   // Find the closing ] for timestamp - look for pattern that ends with proper timestamp format
1809   // Valid timestamp: HH:MM:SS.UUUUUU (time with microseconds, no date)
1810   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1811     p++;
1812   }
1813   if (*p != ']') {
1814     return 0; // Malformed - no closing bracket
1815   }
1816 
1817   size_t timestamp_len = p - timestamp_start;
1818   char timestamp[64];
1819   if (timestamp_len >= sizeof(timestamp) || timestamp_len == 0) {
1820     return 0; // Invalid timestamp - empty or too long
1821   }
1822   SAFE_STRNCPY(timestamp, timestamp_start, timestamp_len);
1823   timestamp[timestamp_len] = '\0';
1824   p++; // Skip ]
1825 
1826   // Skip whitespace after timestamp
1827   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1828     p++;
1829   }
1830 
1831   // Extract level [LEVEL]
1832   if (*p != '[') {
1833     return 0; // Missing opening bracket for level
1834   }
1835   p++;
1836   const char *level_start = p;
1837   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1838     p++;
1839   }
1840   if (*p != ']') {
1841     return 0; // Missing closing bracket for level
1842   }
1843   size_t level_len = p - level_start;
1844   char level_str[16];
1845   if (level_len >= sizeof(level_str) || level_len == 0) {
1846     return 0; // Level string too long or empty
1847   }
1848   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1849   strncpy(level_str, level_start, level_len);
1850   level_str[level_len] = '\0';
1851 
1852   // Determine log level for color selection
1853   log_level_t level = LOG_INFO; // Default
1854   if (strstr(level_str, "DEV") || strstr(level_str, "DEBUG")) {
1855     level = LOG_DEBUG;
1856   } else if (strstr(level_str, "INFO")) {
1857     level = LOG_INFO;
1858   } else if (strstr(level_str, "WARN")) {
1859     level = LOG_WARN;
1860   } else if (strstr(level_str, "ERROR")) {
1861     level = LOG_ERROR;
1862   } else if (strstr(level_str, "FATAL")) {
1863     level = LOG_FATAL;
1864   }
1865 
1866   p++; // Skip ]
1867 
1868   // Skip whitespace after level
1869   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1870     p++;
1871   }
1872 
1873   // Extract thread ID [tid:THREAD_ID] - optional field
1874   uint64_t tid = 0;
1875   if (*p == '[' && strncmp(p, "[tid:", 5) == 0) {
1876     p += 5;
1877     char *tid_end = NULL;
1878     tid = strtoull(p, &tid_end, 10);
1879     if (!tid_end || *tid_end != ']') {
1880       // tid parsing failed, but continue anyway (might still recover)
1881       // Try to find the closing bracket
1882       while (*p && *p != ']') {
1883         p++;
1884       }
1885       if (*p == ']') {
1886         p++;
1887       }
1888     } else {
1889       p = tid_end + 1; // Skip past the ]
1890     }
1891     // Skip whitespace after tid
1892     while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1893       p++;
1894     }
1895   }
1896   // tid is optional - continue parsing if not present
1897 
1898   // Extract file path (everything up to :LINE)
1899   const char *file_start = p;
1900   while (*p && *p != ':') {
1901     p++;
1902   }
1903   if (*p != ':') {
1904     return 0; // Malformed
1905   }
1906   size_t file_len = p - file_start;
1907   char file_path[256];
1908   if (file_len >= sizeof(file_path)) {
1909     return 0;
1910   }
1911   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1912   strncpy(file_path, file_start, file_len);
1913   file_path[file_len] = '\0';
1914   p++; // Skip :
1915 
1916   // Extract line number (digits only)
1917   int line_num = 0;
1918   const char *line_start = p;
1919   while (*p && isdigit((unsigned char)*p)) {
1920     p++;
1921   }
1922   if (p == line_start) {
1923     return 0; // No line number
1924   }
1925   line_num = (int)strtol(line_start, NULL, 10);
1926 
1927   // Skip whitespace and find "in" keyword (be very lenient)
1928   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r')) {
1929     p++;
1930   }
1931 
1932   // Try to find "in " - if not found, might still be valid, just harder to parse
1933   if (strncmp(p, "in ", 3) == 0) {
1934     p += 3; // Skip "in "
1935   } else if (*p == 'i' && *(p + 1) == 'n' && (*(p + 2) == ' ' || *(p + 2) == '\t')) {
1936     // Allow tab after "in"
1937     p += 2; // Skip "in"
1938     while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t')) {
1939       p++;
1940     }
1941   } else {
1942     // Missing "in" keyword - this is a format error
1943     return 0;
1944   }
1945 
1946   // Extract function name (everything up to "()")
1947   const char *func_start = p;
1948   while (*p && *p != '(') {
1949     p++;
1950   }
1951   if (*p != '(' || func_start == p) {
1952     return 0; // Missing function name or parentheses
1953   }
1954   size_t func_len = p - func_start;
1955 
1956   // Trim trailing whitespace from function name
1957   while (func_len > 0 && (func_start[func_len - 1] == ' ' || func_start[func_len - 1] == '\t')) {
1958     func_len--;
1959   }
1960 
1961   char func_name[256];
1962   if (func_len >= sizeof(func_name)) {
1963     func_len = sizeof(func_name) - 1;
1964   }
1965   if (func_len > 0) {
1966     /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1967     strncpy(func_name, func_start, func_len);
1968   }
1969   func_name[func_len] = '\0';
1970 
1971   // Skip "(" and ")" - be lenient about what's between them
1972   if (*p == '(') {
1973     p++;
1974     while (*p && *p != ')') {
1975       p++;
1976     }
1977     if (*p == ')') {
1978       p++;
1979     }
1980   }
1981 
1982   // Skip whitespace and optional colon(s) and other separators
1983   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == ':' || *p == '\t')) {
1984     p++;
1985   }
1986 
1987   // Remaining is the message
1988   const char *message = p;
1989 
1990   // Format is valid, get colors from logging system
1991   const char **colors = log_get_color_array();
1992   if (!colors) {
1993     // No colors available, return plain text
1994     static bool warned_once = false;
1995     if (!warned_once) {
1996       log_debug("WARNING: log_recolor_plain_entry() called but colors not initialized - returning plain text");
1997       warned_once = true;
1998     }
1999     size_t len = strlen(plain_line);
2000     if (len >= buf_size) {
2001       return 0;
2002     }
2003     SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, plain_line, buf_size - 1);
2004     colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2005     return len;
2006   }
2007 
2008   // Build colored output
2009   const char *level_color = colors[level];
2010   const char *reset = colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET];
2011   const char *file_color = colors[1]; // DEBUG/Cyan
2012   const char *line_color = colors[6]; // GREY (matching tid)
2013   const char *func_color = colors[0]; // DEV/Orange
2014   const char *tid_color = colors[6];  // GREY
2015 
2016   int len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2017                           "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp,
2018                           reset, level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color,
2019                           file_path, reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, message);
2020 
2021   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer)) {
2022     return 0;
2023   }
2024 
2025   // Colorize the message part
2026   const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(message);
2027   len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2028                       "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp, reset,
2029                       level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color, file_path,
2030                       reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, colorized_msg);
2031 
2032   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer) || len >= (int)buf_size) {
2033     return 0;
2034   }
2035 
2036   SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, work_buffer, buf_size - 1);
2037   colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2038   return (size_t)len;
2039 }






References colorize_log_message(), file_path,
    log_get_color_array(), and safe_snprintf().

1531   // Detect if not initialized, or if we're using defaults (not reliably detected)
1532   // This ensures we get proper detection after logging is ready, replacing any defaults
1533   // Once we have reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent
1534   if (!g_log.terminal_caps_initialized || !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {

1537     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = false;
1538     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = true;
1539

697                                            {
698   // Respect --quiet flag: if quiet is set, never enable terminal output
699   // But allow disabling even if options are unavailable (for shutdown cleanup)
700 
701   // Only check quiet flag when ENABLING output to avoid deadlock with
702   // concurrent logging from other threads (options_get() can deadlock when
703   // called while other threads are actively logging due to RCU synchronization)
704   if (enabled) {
705     const options_t *opts = options_get();
706     if (opts && opts->quiet) {
707       return; // Silently ignore attempts to enable terminal output when --quiet is set
708     }
709   }
710

void
  log_system_destroy (void )


Shutdown the logging system internal state (system-level cleanup).
    Clears registered session log buffer. Color cleanup is handled separately in
    asciichat_shared_destroy() after memory reporting. Must be called
    once at program shutdown AFTER log_destroy(). Safe to call multiple
    times (idempotent).

void
  log_system_init (void )


Initialize the logging system internal state (system-level init).
